ref: You could have at least put my money in the bank, so I could have earned interest on it.”
text: Ne̱ e̱me̱ne̱ ya waa se̱ ne̱ fo̱ mo̱ŋ taa mo̱ atanne̱‑ɔ yaa bo̱ be̱ya baŋke̱‑rɔ, na mo̱ a kiŋŋi ba‑ɔ na ŋ nya atanne̱ be̱e̱ bo̱ dɔŋŋɔ se̱?’
translated: What do you want to say to me? I want to know what you want to tell me.

ref: The disciples left and found the donkey tied near a door that faced the street. While they were untying it,
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ baa yɔ mfe̱ŋ ya ŋu fe̱yɛ ɔko̱ a ŋure mò̱ kuruma kibunogyi ase̱ kabo̱re̱ se̱. Ne̱ baa me̱raa se̱ ba saŋŋe̱.
translated: Amose, I will tell you what I saw. I saw a great crowd of people gathered together. They were all dressed in white robes and they were singing and dancing. I was amazed by their joy and their peace.

ref: Jesus said, “You people are too stubborn to have any faith! How much longer must I be with you? Why do I have to put up with you? Bring the boy here.”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, “Ke̱se̱bɔrɔle̱ŋ awuye, mo̱ne̱ maa kɔɔre̱ mo̱ mo̱ne̱ i gyi. Nsu mfe̱ne̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ mo̱ aa mo̱ne̱ a kye̱na? Bɛɛɛ nsu mfe̱ne̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ ŋ nyite mo̱ne̱ pwɛɛ na mo̱nꞌ dɛɛ kɔɔre̱ mo̱ gyi‑o ne̱e̱? Mo̱nꞌ taa kayaagyi‑o baa mo̱ ase̱ mfe̱e̱.”
translated: Jesus said to them, “But what about the Sabbath? It is lawful to do good on the Sabbath, is it not? And if your ox or donkey falls into a pit on the Sabbath, will you not immediately pull it out?  How much more valuable is a person than an ox or donkey! Therefore, it is lawful to do good on the Sabbath.”

ref: “I feel sorry for these people. They have been with me for three days, and they don't have anything to eat.
text: “Mo̱ne̱ ke̱e̱ ase̱sɛ de̱maŋte̱ mɔ aaa? Bamo̱ ase̱ŋ bo̱ mo̱ e̱wɛɛ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ bamo̱ a ba mo̱ ase̱ mfe̱e̱ bo̱ nu mo̱ atɔ ke̱kaapo̱‑ɔ, ŋke nsa ne̱e̱. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ, bamo̱ ateese pɛɛɛ a lo̱we̱.
translated: What is the name of the place you are going to? I don't know where you are going, but I will follow you there.  I will go wherever you go.

ref: When King Herod heard about this, he was worried, and so was everyone else in Jerusalem.
text: Owure Hɛrɔd a nu amo̱‑ɔ, ne̱ mò̱ aa Yɛro̱salɛm awuye pɛɛɛ iduŋ a te̱ŋ bamo̱.
translated: King Herod was angry, and he sent to Jerusalem and put to death all the male infants who were under two years old.

ref: So the evil spirits begged Jesus, “Send us into those pigs! Let us go into them.”
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ibrisi amo̱ a ko̱re̱ Yeesuu fe̱yɛ, “Sa a ane̱ ya kye̱na aprako mɔ se̱.”
translated: Amos said to me, "I will show you something about Jesus."

ref: John's followers took his body and buried it. Then they told Jesus what had happened. Jesus Feeds Five Thousand (Mark
text: Ne̱ Yohanee a agyase̱po̱‑ɔ bo̱ko̱ a bo̱ so̱rɔ mò̱ kifuniŋ‑o yaa pure. Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ baa ya tɔwe̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ kaa waa Yohanee‑o gywii Yeesuu. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a sa ase̱sɛ ŋkpe̱ŋ nnuu ateese‑o (Maak
translated: John came baptizing in the wilderness, preaching a baptism of repentance for the forgiveness of sins. As it is written in the prophet Isaiah: "A voice of one crying in the wilderness, 'Prepare the way of the Lord; make straight in the desert a highway for our God.'"

text: Korintoo awuye
translated: Corinthians

ref: Jesus went with his disciples to a place called Gethsemane. When they got there, he told them, “Sit here while I go over there and pray.”
text: Amo̱‑ɔ kamɛɛ‑rɔ, ne̱ Yeesuu mò̱ aa mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ a ba bane̱ ko̱ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Gɛte̱sɛmane̱e̱‑ɔ‑rɔ. Ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱nꞌ kye̱na mfe̱e̱, na ŋ yɔ nno̱ŋ ya ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱.”
translated: And it came to pass, that Jesus went out as usual to the Mount of Olives, and his disciples followed him. And when he came to the place, he said to them, “You know that in two days the Passover is coming, and the Son of Man will be betrayed into the hands of sinners.”

ref: Jesus answered, “Only prayer can force out this kind of demon.” Jesus Again Speaks about His Death (Matthew
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ be̱ŋŋaa bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Sɛye̱ maa taare̱ a a gya ane̱ŋ a ibrisi‑o, amɔ mo̱ne̱ a ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ [ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a kra ako̱ŋ] pwɛɛ.” Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a be̱e̱ tɔwe̱ mò̱ lowi ase̱ŋ‑ɔ (Matiyo
translated: “If you are going to be a disciple of mine, you must deny yourself, take up your cross, and follow me. For whoever wants to save their life will lose it, but whoever loses their life for me will find it.” This is what Jesus said to them.

ref: “That is what you say!” Jesus answered. “But I tell all of you, ‘Soon you will see the Son of Man sitting at the right side of God All-Powerful and coming on the clouds of heaven.’ ”
text: Ŋkee fe̱raa ne̱ Yeesuu a lee kanɔ fe̱yɛ, “Ane̱ŋ ne̱ a du‑o dɛɛ ne̱ fo̱ a tɔwe̱‑ɔ. Ke̱tɔ ne̱ ki tii si‑o e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ saŋ mo̱ne̱ i ŋu mo̱, dimaadi mò̱ gyi‑o, a te Bo̱-e̱le̱ŋ‑ɔ‑kyo̱ŋ‑ɔke̱maa-Bware̱ kigyise si, na mo̱ aa mò̱ a gyi mò̱ kuwure dabe̱‑ɔ, na mo̱ a le̱e̱ awo̱re̱‑rɔ mo̱ e̱ ba.”
translated: “And Jesus said to them, ‘Truly I tell you, unless you change and become like little children, you will never enter the kingdom of heaven. Whoever humbles themselves like this child is the greatest in the kingdom of heaven. And whoever receives one such child in my name receives me; but whoever causes one of these little ones who believe in me to sin, it would be better for him to have a great millstone fastened around his neck and to be drowned in the depth of the sea.”

ref: Then Jesus said to the officer, “You may go home now. Your faith has made it happen.” Right then his servant was healed. Jesus Heals Many People (Mark
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii soogyaa amo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Nare̱ pe̱, na ke̱tɔ ne̱ fo̱ a kɔɔre̱ gyi‑o e̱ waa a ke̱ sa fo̱.” Saŋ ko̱ŋko̱ amo̱‑rɔ dɛɛ ne̱ soogyaa amo̱ a ke̱yaafɔre̱‑ɔ a kpaare̱. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a kya alɔpo̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ‑ɔ (Maak
translated: And Jesus said to them, “Truly I tell you, unless you change and become like little children, you will never enter the kingdom of heaven. Therefore whoever takes the lowly position of this child is the greatest in the kingdom of heaven.

ref: Some other seeds fell where thornbushes grew up and choked the plants.
text: Ayaabraagyi‑o ako̱ ne̱ aa le̱e̱ da e̱fa‑rɔ‑ɔ mɔ a ko̱so̱ daŋ. Amaa e̱fa a daŋ mwiire amo̱ se̱.
translated: I am going to the market to buy some food. I will come back when I am finished.

ref: The chief priests and the whole council wanted to put Jesus to death. So they tried to find some people who would tell lies about him in court.
text: Ne̱ Wuribware̱ alɔŋŋɔpo̱ abre̱sɛ‑ɔ na agyaŋŋe̱po̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ a buwi kpa kanɔte̱ŋ ne̱ ba ye̱re̱ se̱ na Yeesuu gyi ke̱pɔ a bo̱ nya mɔɔ mò̱‑ɔ.
translated: We are not ashamed of the gospel, because it is the power of God that brings salvation to everyone who believes.

ref: Herod was very sorry for what he had said. But he did not want to break the promise he had made in front of his guests.
text: A mo̱ŋ gyi owure‑o akatɔ pɛɛɛ, amaa ane̱ŋ ne̱ ɔɔ kyɔ waa e̱taŋ gye̱maŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ se̱‑ɔ, ɔ mo̱ŋ taare̱ kine.
translated: He went to the market to buy something, but he did not find anything he liked, so he went home empty-handed.

ref: Everyone in town came out to meet Jesus. When they saw him, they begged him to leave their part of the country.
text: Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ ɔke̱maa ne̱ o te maŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ a le̱e̱ ya gyaŋŋaa Yeesuu. Bamo̱ a ŋu mò̱‑ɔ, ne̱ baa ko̱re̱ mò̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ le̱e̱ bamo̱ ifuri si kiŋŋi yɔ mfe̱ŋ ne̱ ɔɔ le̱e̱ ba‑ɔ.
translated: I am the one who was sent by God to bring you the good news of salvation. I have come to bring you light and to show you the way to God. I have come to save you from your sins.

ref: and when I was naked, you gave me clothes to wear. When I was sick, you took care of me, and when I was in jail, you visited me.”
text: Saŋ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a ŋu fe̱yɛ m buŋ aprakɛɛ‑ɔ, ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a sa mo̱ atɔ, ne̱ mo̱ a buŋ. Saŋ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a ŋu mo̱ na mo̱ e̱ lɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a kya mo̱‑rɔ. Saŋ ne̱ baa tii mo̱ tiikpa‑ɔ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a bo̱ laa mo̱.’
translated: If you are a good person, you will not be harmed. If you are not good, you will be harmed. If you are good, you will be protected. If you are not good, you will be destroyed. If you are good, you will live long.

ref: Jesus answered: You are completely wrong! You don't know what the Scriptures teach. And you don't know anything about the power of God.
text: Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a be̱ŋŋaa bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ a fo̱ kpa, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ nyi abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ ase̱ŋ, ne̱ mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ nyi ane̱ŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ ke̱yaale̱ŋ du‑o, ane̱ŋ dɛɛ mo̱ne̱ maa nu ke̱mo̱ kaase̱.
translated: When Jesus saw the people, he was moved with compassion for them, because they were harassed and helpless, like sheep without a shepherd. Then he said to his disciples, "The harvest is plentiful, but the workers are few.

ref: “Get up! Pick up your mat and go on home.”
text: “Ke̱e̱ ɛ, ko̱so̱, taa fo̱ kraŋ a fo̱ nare̱ yɔ pe̱.”
translated: Yes, I will go and get some food for you.

ref: Brothers and sisters will betray one another and have each other put to death. Parents will betray their own children, and children will turn against their parents and have them killed.
text: Saŋ amo̱ dɛɛ na se̱sɛ yaa mò̱ daa bɛɛɛ mò̱ tire maŋ ayaale̱ŋpo̱ akatɔ‑rɔ a bo̱ mɔɔ mò̱. Na se̱ mɔ yaa mò̱ gyi maŋ ayaale̱ŋpo̱ akatɔ‑rɔ na bo̱ mɔɔ mò̱. Na ŋyaagyi mɔ ye̱re̱ bo̱ kye bamo̱ ase̱ na anyi yaa bamo̱ maŋ ayaale̱ŋpo̱ akatɔ‑rɔ a bo̱ mɔɔ.
translated: I want to tell you something. I want to tell you that the way you are doing things is not good. I want to tell you that you should not do things that way. I want to tell you that you should do things the right way.
